 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","title":"Add deterministic leak-oracle regressions for churn, reconnect, shutdown, restore, and workflow storms","description":"# Goal
Turn the leak and lifecycle fixes into deterministic regression tests that fail before future contributors reintroduce the same classes of breakage.

This task exists because long-haul issues are expensive to rediscover by soak alone. The finish-line program needs deterministic tests that model the dangerous lifecycle patterns directly.","design":"# Design
1. Build targeted churn scenarios for pane creation and teardown, reconnect, detach, shutdown, restore, and workflow-storm style activity.
2. Use deterministic runtime or focused harnesses so the results are reproducible.
3. Assert on leak-oracle signals from the instrumentation layer rather than only on functional output.
4. Keep the scenarios close enough to real workloads that later soak work benefits from the same invariants.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered lifecycle regressions have deterministic tests that fail when retention or cleanup behavior regresses.
2. The tests assert on relevant counters, ownership signals, or observable cleanup outcomes.
3. The suite is repeatable and suitable for regular remote verification.
4. Completion evidence records representative test runs and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"Soak can tell us the system got sick. This bead is what makes the common failure classes cheap to catch earlier.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.541457Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:46:12.484873Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:46:12.484676Z","close_reason":"Deterministic leak-oracle regressions added; evidence at tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.4.4/leak_oracle_regressions/20260419T163650Z (remote run blocked by pre-existing runtime_compat tokio::select import failure before new tests executed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","oracles","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:04.891706Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.541457Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.498175Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:40.784385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","title":"Build the long-haul 50, 100, and 200-pane swarm soak harness and benchmark matrix","description":"# Goal
Create the workload harness that proves FrankenTerm can actually handle the large multi-agent operating model it was built for.

This task exists because the finish line is not satisfied by unit tests and short integration runs. The project needs a representative long-haul harness that exercises the control plane, mux, scrollback, search, and operator paths under realistic swarm pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Define representative 50, 100, and 200-pane workload profiles, including idle periods, bursty output, reconnects, search, capture, and policy-sensitive actions.
2. Build a soak harness that supports both shorter developer-smoke runs and release-scale long-haul runs so the workload definitions get exercised regularly instead of only at release time.
3. Record benchmark-style metrics and artifacts during the soak so later gates can reason about trend lines rather than a single pass fail.
4. Keep the harness operator-readable, segmentable, and repeatable enough for future release cycles and incident follow-up.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A repeatable soak harness exists for representative multi-agent workloads at 50, 100, and 200 pane scale.
2. The harness records enough metrics and artifacts to diagnose failures without local guesswork.
3. The harness is tied to concrete performance and stability expectations rather than being a best-effort stress script.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative run record and artifact bundle layout.
5. The harness includes a shorter smoke profile and a release-scale long-haul profile so the same workload model is usable in both frequent validation and deep proof runs.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the bead that transforms the user’s original mission statement into an executable test campaign.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:02:07.347208Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:02:07.347037Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","performance","reliability","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.148984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.506745Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.420840Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.729476Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.086328Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","title":"Turn runtime SLOs, soak confidence, and performance budgets into hard release gates","description":"# Goal
Convert reliability and performance evidence into explicit release gates.

This task exists because data that is not attached to a gate will be ignored the next time the schedule gets tight. The finish-line story needs hard go or no-go criteria for stability, leak behavior, and performance budgets.","design":"# Design
1. Define the SLO and budget thresholds that matter for the finish line.
2. Connect those thresholds to the instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak outputs from earlier beads.
3. Make failure modes explicit and actionable so the gates are useful rather than ceremonial.
4. Record the resulting gate definitions in bead-native form for release work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reliability and performance budgets are explicit and machine-checkable or procedure-checkable.
2. The release process has named gates for leak behavior, soak confidence, and relevant performance metrics.
3. Gate failures produce actionable diagnostics.
4. Release-readiness tasks can rely on these gates rather than reinventing them.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is what prevents multi-day swarm reliability from devolving back into a vibes-based claim.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:21:34.302865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:21:34.302591Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","release-readiness","reliability","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.955060Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.766069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.385704Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.5","title":"[EPIC] Release readiness, operator polish, and permanent regression guards","description":"# Goal
Turn the completed technical work into a ship-ready, operator-trustworthy project: final docs, guards, tuning guidance, end-to-end acceptance scenarios, and a closure bundle that future contributors can rely on.

This epic exists because a project can be technically improved and still not be finish-line ready if docs lie, CI does not defend the invariants, or no one has captured the evidence and workflows that define ready for real multi-agent use.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the narrative around the final native architecture and honest supported behavior.
2. Add permanent machine checks for the invariants this program establishes.
3. Expose live health and incident-diagnostics surfaces so operators can actually use the reliability work under pressure.
4. Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints so the finished system is operable from the first real interaction.
5. Validate the original mission workflows end to end, not just unit by unit.
6. Package the result into a final closure bundle with explicit remaining risks, if any.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Docs and operator guidance describe the final system rather than a migration-in-progress story.
2. CI, doctor, and audit surfaces fail fast when `tokio`, `smol`, or fake supported capabilities creep back in.
3. Operator acceptance scenarios covering the original mission are executed with artifacts.
4. A final release-candidate checklist and evidence bundle exist in bead-native form.
5. Operator-facing health and diagnostic workflows are first-class outputs of this epic, not incidental notes.
6. First-run setup and recovery entrypoints are polished enough to be part of the supported operator story rather than an undocumented initiation ritual.","notes":"This epic is what prevents the repo from sliding back into technically interesting but operationally ambiguous. It closes the loop between implementation, proof, and operator trust.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"cod3","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:10.948935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","goal-line","operator","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","title":"Rewrite README, AGENTS, and operator docs around the final native Asupersync architecture and honest support matrix","description":"# Goal
Make the repository narrative match the finished system.

This task exists because docs that still describe migration debt, placeholder capabilities, or outdated runtime assumptions will immediately undermine the credibility of the finish-line push.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the high-level architecture story around the final native Asupersync model.
2. Update AGENTS guidance and operator docs to reflect the real supported capability matrix, verification expectations, and live health or diagnostic surfaces added by this program.
3. Remove or rewrite stale references to transitional architecture that no longer exists.
4. Keep the docs specific enough that future agents can work from them without reopening old planning documents.","acceptance_criteria":"1. README, AGENTS, and core operator docs describe the final system rather than a migration in progress.
2. Runtime, support-matrix, and verification language is accurate and current.
3. Operators and future agents can understand the intended architecture and supported surfaces directly from the docs.
4. The updated docs are cross-linked to the finish-line evidence model where appropriate.
5. Documentation examples, support claims, and operator procedures are validated against the shipped commands, recorded verification artifacts, live diagnostic surfaces, and the first-run or recovery path so the docs cannot drift ahead of reality.","notes":"This bead is the documentation counterpart to the code cleanup. If the narrative stays stale, the project will feel unfinished even after the code improves.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:02:52.633640Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:02:52.633305Z","close_reason":"Docs narrative matches final asupersync-native architecture/support matrix; verified with rch build 29788794240631126 (cargo check -p frankenterm, exit 0) and existing ft-xbnl0.5.1 doc commits at HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.535955Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.715747Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.022385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.471912Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:09.776559Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:14.259339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","title":"Add permanent CI, doctor, and audit guards for zero tokio smol and zero fake supported capabilities","description":"# Goal
Make the finish-line invariants self-defending.

This task exists because the project needs durable protection against two classes of regression: runtime debt creeping back in and fake supported capabilities quietly reappearing.","design":"# Design
1. Add or extend CI checks, doctor surfaces, and audit scripts that defend the no-Tokio no-Smol property and the honest support-matrix property.
2. Keep the checks actionable so failures explain what violated the rule and where.
3. Wire the checks into the normal contributor path instead of leaving them as optional extras.
4. Reuse the evidence and inventories built earlier in the program where possible.
5. Validate not only code invariants but also the verification contract itself where practical, including required artifact manifests, log schema, and deterministic E2E entrypoints.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The key finish-line invariants are defended by permanent automated or semi-automated checks.
2. Contributors can run the relevant checks locally and in CI.
3. Violations are specific enough to fix quickly.
4. The release lane can trust these guards as part of ongoing maintenance.
5. Selected deterministic E2E scripts, artifact manifests, and structured-log expectations are automatically checked or linted so the testing and evidence discipline in this program does not silently decay.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is how the finish-line work stays finished after the first release candidate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341498Z","close_reason":"finish-line guards passing; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","doctor","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.793483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.811867Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.145075Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","title":"Define blessed tuning profiles and operator playbooks for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane fleets","description":"# Goal
Turn the performance and reliability findings into concrete operating guidance.

This task exists because a finished swarm terminal should not leave operators guessing which settings, limits, and practices are appropriate for small, medium, and very large fleets.","design":"# Design
1. Derive tuning profiles from the reliability and soak evidence instead of speculation.
2. Document the practical knobs, warning signs, diagnostic surfaces, and recommended operating patterns for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane deployments.
3. Keep the guidance honest about which workloads are supported and which are still aspirational.
4. Package the result so future operators can use it without reading the whole backlog.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has concrete tuning guidance for representative small, medium, and large fleet sizes.
2. The guidance is tied to measured evidence rather than vague rules of thumb.
3. Operators can use the playbooks and diagnostic surfaces to configure and monitor the system responsibly.
4. The playbooks are integrated with the final docs lane.
5. The playbooks cite exact verification scripts, thresholds, logged artifacts, and escalation paths instead of vague advice.","notes":"This bead turns performance work into something that operators can actually act on.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844353Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete; artifacts docs/ft-xbnl0-5-3-blessed-tuning-validation.json and tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.5.3/blessed_tuning_profiles/20260419T193608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","operator","performance","tuning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.072933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.582153Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:10.265504Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3556,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyTiger claiming docs/playbook slice: blessed fleet profiles + operator guidance docs tied to 4.6 gate artifacts; avoiding benchmark/runtime implementation surfaces.","created_at":"2026-04-19T19:34:05Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-i659","title":"FTUI-06.1 Build keybinding and input parity map (global + per-view)","description":"## Background\nUsers rely on existing keybindings and modal behaviors.\n\n## Deliverables\n- canonical keymap table (global and per-view)\n- mapping implementation with deterministic conflict policy\n- parity tests for navigation and action keys\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- keybinding behavior matches parity contract\n- no ambiguous or conflicting key paths remain.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:24.060123264Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.268288Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:20:33.216743006Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-i659","depends_on_id":"wa-f5wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-i9uw","title":"E2E: perf regression smoke with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Validate performance optimizations end-to-end without regressions, with high-fidelity logs.

## Requirements
- Run a representative high-load scenario (many panes / large transcripts) using existing fixtures.
- Capture timing metrics and verbose logs as artifacts (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS query).
- Assert performance budgets are met and output remains correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E perf smoke passes locally and in CI.
- On failure, artifacts include full logs, timing metrics, and scenario parameters.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:32.127057017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.322539Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:11:31.440116741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-i9uw","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-i9uw","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-i9uw","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-ib09","title":"Tests: dedupe/cooldown/mute correctness and determinism","description":"# Task: Tests for noise control

## Goal
Prevent regressions in dedupe/cooldown/mute logic.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for:
  - dedupe within window
  - cooldown suppression
  - mute TTL expiry
  - escalation thresholds

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests lock behavior and make changes intentional.

-{"id":"wa-iehgn","title":"[EPIC] Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms (200+ panes)","description":"# Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms

## Vision
FrankenTerm managing 200+ concurrent AI agent panes on a 512GB RAM / 64-core server with zero degradation. Every subsystem optimized to handle the unique load profile of agent swarms: bursty output, long-lived sessions, competing builds, and cascading resource exhaustion.

## Methodology — MANDATORY SKILLS
Each implementation bead under this epic MUST invoke the appropriate skill:

1. **/extreme-software-optimization** — Profile-driven optimization with behavior proofs. Use for ALL performance work. Never optimize blind — measure first, prove the fix, measure after.
2. **/alien-artifact-coding** — Elicit deep mathematical knowledge for formal guarantees. Use for lock-free data structures, scheduling algorithms, and any code where correctness under concurrency is critical.
3. **/idea-wizard** — Generate and operationalize improvement ideas. Use when brainstorming novel approaches to scaling problems.

## Load Profile (200+ agent panes)
- **Processes**: 600-2000+ (agents + MCP servers + child builds/tests)
- **File descriptors**: 5000-15000+ (pipes, sockets, files per pane)
- **Memory**: 60-200GB baseline (300MB-1GB per agent)
- **CPU**: Bursty — idle agents near 0%, active builds at 100% per core
- **I/O**: Mixed — agents reading/writing files, competing cargo builds thrashing disk
- **Network**: Moderate — MCP, HTTP, Unix socket IPC

## Key Insight from /system-performance-remediation
The #1 cause of swarm performance meltdowns is NOT individual pane overhead — it's **cascading resource exhaustion**: one stuck agent spawns competing builds, which starve other agents of CPU, which causes them to timeout and retry, spawning MORE builds. The fix is proactive lifecycle management, not faster I/O.

## Architecture Principles
1. **Pressure-aware**: Monitor /proc/pressure/cpu,memory,io and react BEFORE meltdown
2. **Lifecycle-managed**: Every pane has age/activity tracking with automatic cleanup policies
3. **Coordinated**: Agents share build caches, coordinate resource usage via FrankenTerm
4. **Formally verified**: Critical data structures proven correct under concurrency (/alien-artifact-coding)
5. **Profile-driven**: Never optimize without profiling first (/extreme-software-optimization)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"cod3","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:28.451043Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:28:10.680708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-1sm78","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.681162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-1ta8j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:51.806372Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-27hy6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.261260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-2ahu0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.390667Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-2oph2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.517475Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-8eoug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.132290Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-9i3rh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:51.972943Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"wa-iehgn","title":"[EPIC] Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms (200+ panes)","description":"# Ultra-Performance FrankenTerm for Massive Agent Swarms

## Vision
FrankenTerm managing 200+ concurrent AI agent panes on a 512GB RAM / 64-core server with zero degradation. Every subsystem optimized to handle the unique load profile of agent swarms: bursty output, long-lived sessions, competing builds, and cascading resource exhaustion.

## Methodology — MANDATORY SKILLS
Each implementation bead under this epic MUST invoke the appropriate skill:

1. **/extreme-software-optimization** — Profile-driven optimization with behavior proofs. Use for ALL performance work. Never optimize blind — measure first, prove the fix, measure after.
2. **/alien-artifact-coding** — Elicit deep mathematical knowledge for formal guarantees. Use for lock-free data structures, scheduling algorithms, and any code where correctness under concurrency is critical.
3. **/idea-wizard** — Generate and operationalize improvement ideas. Use when brainstorming novel approaches to scaling problems.

## Load Profile (200+ agent panes)
- **Processes**: 600-2000+ (agents + MCP servers + child builds/tests)
- **File descriptors**: 5000-15000+ (pipes, sockets, files per pane)
- **Memory**: 60-200GB baseline (300MB-1GB per agent)
- **CPU**: Bursty — idle agents near 0%, active builds at 100% per core
- **I/O**: Mixed — agents reading/writing files, competing cargo builds thrashing disk
- **Network**: Moderate — MCP, HTTP, Unix socket IPC

## Key Insight from /system-performance-remediation
The #1 cause of swarm performance meltdowns is NOT individual pane overhead — it's **cascading resource exhaustion**: one stuck agent spawns competing builds, which starve other agents of CPU, which causes them to timeout and retry, spawning MORE builds. The fix is proactive lifecycle management, not faster I/O.

## Architecture Principles
1. **Pressure-aware**: Monitor /proc/pressure/cpu,memory,io and react BEFORE meltdown
2. **Lifecycle-managed**: Every pane has age/activity tracking with automatic cleanup policies
3. **Coordinated**: Agents share build caches, coordinate resource usage via FrankenTerm
4. **Formally verified**: Critical data structures proven correct under concurrency (/alien-artifact-coding)
5. **Profile-driven**: Never optimize without profiling first (/extreme-software-optimization)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"cod3","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:28.451043Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:29:14.213097Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:29:14.212803Z","close_reason":"All child ultra-performance beads are closed; epic is stale wrapper state only.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-1sm78","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.681162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-1ta8j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:51.806372Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-27hy6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.261260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-2ahu0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.390667Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-2oph2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.517475Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-8eoug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:52.132290Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-iehgn","depends_on_id":"wa-9i3rh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:51.972943Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-iqf","title":"E2E integration checklist: comprehensive coverage validation by feature","description":"# E2E Integration Checklist: Comprehensive coverage validation

## Purpose
Ensure every major feature has **end-to-end coverage** with:
- deterministic synchronization (no fixed sleeps)
- detailed, structured logging
- artifact bundles that make failures diagnosable

This checklist is **human-friendly**.
The **canonical, enforceable** source of truth for “what runs under `--all`” is the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`).

## How to use this checklist
For each item below:
- ensure there is a corresponding E2E case in the registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`)
- ensure the case runs via the standard runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`)
- ensure the case follows the harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`)

If an item is intentionally *not* E2E-tested (e.g., requires human credentials), document the reason and ensure we at least have:
- fixture-based integration tests, and/or
- a manual smoke-test command with stable output and artifact capture

## Checklist by feature area

### Phase 1: Core features

#### Ingest (`wa-4vx.4`)
- [ ] E2E: basic text capture from a real WezTerm dummy pane
- [ ] E2E: delta extraction produces correct segments (no duplication)
- [ ] E2E: FTS indexing enables search
- [ ] Artifacts: segment stats (bytes/lines), ingest lag, FTS timing

#### Pattern detection (`wa-4vx.5`)
- [ ] E2E: known patterns trigger events (at least 1 per critical workflow trigger)
- [ ] E2E: false positives are rejected (near-miss negative fixture)
- [ ] E2E: extraction captures expected structured facts
- [ ] Artifacts: rule_id matched, extracted facts JSON, match timing

#### Daemon/runtime (`wa-4vx.6`)
- [ ] E2E: `wa watch` starts, runs, and exits cleanly
- [ ] E2E: graceful shutdown flushes storage queue and releases lock
- [ ] E2E: restart recovers cleanly (no corrupt state)
- [ ] Artifacts: health snapshot (queues/lag), watcher logs, lock state

#### Robot mode (`wa-4vx.7`)
- [ ] E2E: core robot commands produce valid JSON envelopes
- [ ] E2E: stable error codes for common failures (pane missing, policy denied)
- [ ] E2E: `wa robot send` verification (`--wait-for`) works (or `PaneWaiter` equivalent)
- [ ] Artifacts: raw JSON outputs for each command + schema validation results

#### Safety/policy (`wa-4vx.8`)
- [ ] E2E: policy denies blocked actions (alt-screen, recent gap, prompt not active)
- [ ] E2E: approval allow-once flow works and is audited
- [ ] E2E: audit trail captures allow/deny decisions with redaction
- [ ] Artifacts: audit export slice, redaction proof scans (no secrets)

### Phase 2: Workflows

#### Workflow engine (`wa-nu4.1.1`)
- [ ] E2E: workflow triggered by an event under `--auto-handle`
- [ ] E2E: workflow step logging is complete and ordered
- [ ] E2E: workflow resumes after restart (idempotent; no duplicate sends)
- [ ] Artifacts: workflow execution logs + step log export

#### Usage limits (`wa-nu4.1.3`)
- [ ] E2E: end-to-end usage-limit workflow **fixture-first** (no real auth)
- [ ] E2E: key failure modes are safe and actionable (MFA required, cannot pick account)
- [ ] Artifacts: parsed resume/session info (redacted), next-step plan output

#### Compaction (`wa-nu4.1.2`)
- [ ] E2E: compaction detected and handled exactly once per event (dedupe/cooldown)
- [ ] E2E: injected context is verified (echo/marker)
- [ ] Artifacts: detection evidence, injection payload preview (redacted), verification tail hash

### Phase 3–4: Polish / integration

#### Diagnostics/health/metrics (`wa-nu4.3.4`)
- [ ] E2E: `wa doctor` healthy vs broken output is actionable
- [ ] E2E: metrics endpoint (Prometheus) responds when enabled (`wa-nu4.3.4.5`)
- [ ] E2E: watcher health snapshot is visible via CLI (`wa-nu4.3.4.2`)
- [ ] Artifacts: diag bundle layout + redaction proofs (`wa-nu4.3.4.7`, `wa-nu4.3.4.8`)

#### Notifications (`wa-psm`)
- [ ] E2E: events trigger notifications (webhook mock server)
- [ ] E2E: throttling prevents spam
- [ ] E2E: failure recovery works (network down, endpoint 500)
- [ ] Artifacts: delivery attempts, retry/backoff, persisted notification history

#### CLI polish (`wa-rnf`)
- [ ] E2E: shell completion generation works (at least smoke)
- [ ] E2E: alias expansion is correct
- [ ] E2E: help text stays accurate (docs-smoke style)

#### Timeline/correlation (`wa-6sk`)
- [ ] E2E: events appear in timeline
- [ ] E2E: correlations are deterministic on fixtures
- [ ] E2E: query performance acceptable under a seeded dataset

#### Quick-fix suggestions (`wa-bnm`)
- [ ] E2E: suggestions appear on common errors/events
- [ ] E2E: suggestions are copy-pasteable and safe
- [ ] Artifacts: suggestion IDs fired + dismissal persistence (if applicable)

## E2E case requirements (non-negotiable)
- No fixed `sleep N` synchronization; use wait-for conditions with timeouts.
- All cases write an artifacts directory and print its path on failure.
- Every case prints a PASS/FAIL summary with elapsed time and key assertions.

## Registry
- Not a case. This checklist validates that registry coverage matches feature coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every checklist item maps to a registry case (or is explicitly justified as non-E2E)
- [ ] All cases follow `wa-4vx.10.6` contract and run under `wa-4vx.10.11`
- [ ] CI runs `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all --verbose` and uploads artifacts on failure (`wa-nu4.3.9.6`)

## Testing
- Meta: add a lightweight “checklist-to-registry” validator that fails if this checklist references removed epics/tasks or if required areas are missing from the registry.
- Ensure the validator itself is deterministic and runs in CI.
